name: docker-php-formatter
description: Format PHP files using PHP CS Fixer running inside Docker containers. Supports ${workspaceFolder} variables — configure once globally, works for all projects.

phpCsFixerDocker.container.markdownDescription:
Name of the Docker container or docker-compose service to use. You can use the **Select Container** command to pick from running containers. Supports `${env:VAR}` variables.

Example: `php`, `app`, `${env:APP_NAME}-php`

phpCsFixerDocker.envFile.markdownDescription:
Path to a `.env` file used to resolve `${env:VAR}` variables in other settings. Supports `${workspaceFolder}`.

Example: `${workspaceFolder}/.env`

phpCsFixerDocker.pathMapping.markdownDescription:
Path mapping between your local workspace and the container path, in `host:container` format.

Supports `${workspaceFolder}` and `${workspaceFolderBasename}` variables.

Example: `${workspaceFolder}:/var/www/html`

phpCsFixerDocker.phpCsFixerPath.markdownDescription:
Path to `php-cs-fixer` inside the container. Can be relative to the container path mapping, or absolute.

Examples:
- `vendor/bin/php-cs-fixer` (Composer install)
- `/usr/local/bin/php-cs-fixer` (global install)

phpCsFixerDocker.configFile.markdownDescription: Name of the PHP CS Fixer config file. If empty, the extension auto-detects common filenames (`.php-cs-fixer.dist.php`, `.php-cs-fixer.php`, `.php_cs.dist`, `.php_cs`).

phpCsFixerDocker.rules.markdownDescription:
Additional PHP CS Fixer rules in JSON format. Only used when no config file is found.

Example: `{"@PSR12": true, "array_syntax": {"syntax": "short"}}`

phpCsFixerDocker.localFallback.description: If true, falls back to a local php-cs-fixer installation when no Docker container is running.

phpCsFixerDocker.localPhpCsFixerPath.markdownDescription:
Path to the local `php-cs-fixer` binary (used for fallback). Auto-detected if empty:
1. `${workspaceFolder}/vendor/bin/php-cs-fixer`
2. `php-cs-fixer` in PATH
